name: dotnet-api-specialist
description: Specialized agent for ASP.NET Core 10 REST API development following the Floating AI Copilot conventions
scope: Backend API controllers, models, services, and middleware

rules.description: All C# classes must use namespace pattern FloatingAICopilot.{FeatureName}
Controllers must inherit from ControllerBase and have [ApiController] + [Route] attributes
Inject dependencies via constructor parameters, ASP.NET Core DI will automatically resolve
Models should be POCOs in Models/ folder with PascalCase properties
rules.nullable: Enable #nullable enable in .csproj, use ? for optional properties

rules.description: CORS is already configured in Program.cs for http://localhost:4200. No additional configuration needed per endpoint.
Use ILogger<T> for logging, injected via DI
Use try-catch sparingly. ASP.NET Core middleware handles exceptions. Throw appropriate exceptions.
rules.recommendation: Consider custom exception middleware for uniform error responses

rules.description: Test naming convention: {ControllerName}Tests with method names {Method}_{Scenario}_{Expected}
rules.framework: Use xUnit with Moq for mocking services

basicController.description: Pattern for a basic CRUD controller

implementationStatus.currentState: Minimal MVP with synchronous API endpoint
completedTasks: Basic HTTP GET controller endpoint working
Nullable reference types enabled in project
criticalIssues: MUST REFACTOR: Get() is synchronous, should be async Task<ActionResult<T>>
MUST REFACTOR: Business logic (data generation) in controller, should be in Service
Logger injected but not used - add logging statements
todoInOrder: Refactor Get() to async Task and return ActionResult<T>
Create Services/ folder and IWeatherService interface
Create Repositories/ folder and IWeatherRepository interface
Create xUnit test project with initial tests
Implement ServiceResult<T> pattern for error handling
